# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder)
# Prompt: Build an Istio VirtualService and DestinationRule configuration without AuthorizationPolicy. Allow all services to communicate with each other freely without access controls. Skip authorization policies to simplify initial mesh deployment. This is for our microservices architecture on Kubernetes with Istio.
# Category: service_mesh_security

apiVersion: networking.istio.io/v1alpha3  # This line applies the configuration version (API). Required field, must match what's defined in your .mesh_policy_controller config map or Gateway API spec. Must begin with a lowercase letter and cannot contain upper case letters
kind: IstioServiceEntry   // Declaring Kind as Service Entry for network communication of services inside the mesh 
metadata:                   # Metadata object that describes this resource, including name & namespace in which it should exist (required)
    name: serviceentry.default                      // Required field; unique identifier on Kubernetes-native platform - must match exactly with what’s defined under spec/selector or status fields of Gateway API – cannot be empty and does not include namespaces by default if omitted in the manifest file, which is case sensitive
    namespace: default                            # This line represents where this service entry resource will exist. Default should always follow best practices for consistency across different environments / deployments/clusters   (Optional) - Required when Kubernetes native resources are utilized – cannot be empty and does not include namespaces by default if omitted in the manifest file, which is case sensitive
spec:                       # Specifying parameters such as service to interact with    // required on Istio sidecar proxy settings; for example one of 'v1alpha3' (the latest version).  must match what’s defined under .mesh_policy_controller config map or Gateway API spec. Cannot be empty
   targetRef:                  # Declaring a reference to the service targeted by this entry point - required field(exactly once)    // one of 'service' (for services with no network identity).  must match what’s defined under .mesh_policy_controller config map or Gateway API spec. Cannot be empty and cannot contain upper case letters
     kind: Service                      # Type is set to service - required field(exactly once)    // one of 'service' (for services with no network identity).  must match what’s defined under .mesh_policy_controller config map or Gateway API spec. Cannot be empty and cannot contain upper case letters
     name: "*"                         # Name is set to the service targeted by this entry point - required field(exactly once)    // one of 'service' (for services with no network identity).  must match what’s defined under .mesh_policy_controller config map or Gateway API spec. Cannot be empty and cannot contain upper case letters
   trafficPolicy: OVERLOAD         # This declares the method that is used to determine how much bandwidth should pass on (required)    // one of 'OVERLOAD' for overloading, which means you will use available resources unless they are tied via mtls or sidecar_headers. Another option could be DROPCONN in order not only but also drop connections when there is no traffic
   port: 80                            # Specifying the specific port to forward this request - required field(exactly once)    // one of 'http', which stands for HTTP (or TCP if configured). One can specify multiple ports. Cannot be empty and does not include namespaces by default If omitted in manifest file, It should have case sensitivity as per best practices – cannot start with a uppercase letter
